ai_explanation.simple_explanation: Nico Hülkenberg is ranked P10 for Audi with 31.3% confidence and low trust. The model's likely finish is P13.91, with an expected range of P11-P17. Disagreement is medium, so treat the rank cautiously. Missing signals: pit stop data, practice or lap pace.
ai_explanation.expert_explanation: Nico Hülkenberg: positive signals: pit stop execution, team strategy gain; negative signals: win probability, podium probability; 24 feature groups available; 2 feature groups missing
ai_explanation.risk_summary: DNF risk 29.2%, medium model disagreement, 2 missing feature groups
ai_explanation.upside_case: Upside case: Nico Hülkenberg reaches the front of the projected range (P11-P17) if available strengths translate cleanly. Main support: pit stop execution, team strategy gain.
ai_explanation.downside_case: Downside case: Nico Hülkenberg falls toward the back of the range if missing data or risk flags dominate. Main concerns: win probability, podium probability.
ai_explanation.missing_data_note: Not enough data for pit stop data, practice or lap pace; confidence and trust are penalized.
ai_explanation.model_agreement_note: Model disagreement is medium: top10 rank low points probability.
ai_explanation.source_health_note: Source warnings affect this view: pit stop data, practice or lap pace.
ai_explanation.scenario_note: This is a post-qualifying view; race execution, actual strategy, and incidents are still unknown.
ai_explanation.strategy_note: Expected strategy: 2 stops, first pit around lap 16, compounds medium > hard > medium.

predicted_result.simple_explanation: Nico Hülkenberg is ranked P10 for Audi with 39.0% confidence and low trust. The model's likely finish is P13.91, with an expected range of P11-P17. Disagreement is medium, so treat the rank cautiously. Missing signals: pit stop data, practice or lap pace.
predicted_result.expert_explanation: Nico Hülkenberg: positive signals: pit stop execution, team strategy gain; negative signals: win probability, podium probability; 24 feature groups available; 2 feature groups missing
predicted_result.trust_explanation: Trust is low at 47.5/100, combining model agreement 30.4%, source health 72.1%, data completeness 85.8%, validation strength 39.0%, and stage confidence 72.0%.
cannot_know_factors: safety cars, red flags, crashes, weather shifts, mechanical issues, and late FIA decisions can change outcomes
predicted_result.recommended_interpretation: Use as calibrated probabilities and uncertainty-aware ranking, not a guarantee.

post_race_ai_review.best_call: Best available audited call: Charles Leclerc was within 0 positions.
post_race_ai_review.worst_miss: Worst available miss: George Russell predicted P4 and finished P19.
feature_improvement_suggestions: Review feature groups for drivers with the largest rank error.
Separate car pace from strategy and incident outcomes when post-race data is available.
chaos_factors: safety cars, red flags, reliability, weather, penalties, and traffic can create non-repeatable misses
post_race_ai_review.was_miss_predictable: Partly. Large errors should be treated as review prompts unless source data identifies a repeatable feature gap.

acceptance_rules: finish-position MAE must not regress beyond threshold
top-3 and top-10 recall must not drop beyond threshold
Brier score must not worsen beyond threshold
critical data sources and output contracts must pass validation

warnings: Model comparison is promotion-gate metadata, not proof of guaranteed prediction accuracy.

limitations: Live race adjustments only become official model output when timing data is genuinely fresh and generated contracts are refreshed.
Timing fallback and archived data are labelled as replay/archive, never live telemetry.
2026 Boost and Active Aero fields are explainable proxies until full 2026+ timing and technical evidence exists.
Post-race corrections remain pending until official result rows are available after the configured delay.
F1 predictions cannot be guaranteed because crashes, red flags, failures, penalties, strategy, weather, and source delays are inherently uncertain.
